name: Build an iOS App
description: A native iOS application using SwiftUI

blueprint.name: Xcode
blueprint.blockerMessage: To build an iOS app, you need Xcode installed. It's free from the App Store.

blueprint.name: iOS Simulator
blueprint.note: Downloaded automatically if Xcode is installed but simulator runtimes are missing

blockers.name: Xcode
blockers.resolution: Install Xcode from the App Store, then come back

authRequirements.name: Apple Developer Account
authRequirements.note: Only needed for App Store publishing or testing on a physical device. Simulator testing works without it.

scaffolding.note: Ship a minimal SwiftUI app template in blueprints/templates/ios-app/. Copy it to the project directory. This gives us a working .xcodeproj with SwiftUI App lifecycle, unlike swift package init which only creates a CLI executable.
postScaffold: Rename TemplateApp references to project name
Set up basic ContentView.swift with placeholder

initialContext: The user wants to build an iOS app. Ask what the app does (one sentence), who it's for, and the 2-3 most important screens. Don't ask about technical details — the architect handles those. Then hand off to architect for app architecture, designer for UI/UX, and mobile engineer for implementation.

ticketTemplate.title: UI design system and screen mockups
Build core screens
dependsOn: UI design system and screen mockups
